subject: rtl8821cu: workaround for bad efuse in EDUP EP-AC1661.
author: Sorgelig
provenance_doc_ref: patch-provenance.md:389 — grouped row (9 commits: 33ff5146a, 3740d5b88, 2371fb1aa, 143ce187e, 4e98a68d1, 43fbb63ae, 993b82e31, 115b1d1ae, fc09a292a). Text: 'Verify the two local device fixes are present upstream or re-apply as package patches.' Note: this is a conditional TODO, not an assertion that the fix IS upstream — so there is nothing in it to literally 'agree'/'disagree' with; verification below resolves the open question (answer: NOT upstream, re-apply-as-patch branch applies).

impact.effect_if_absent: EFUSE-corrupted rtl8821c-family adapters (map byte at offset 0xCA reads 0xFF/unprogrammed) get efuse->rfe_option = 0xFF & 0x1f = 0x1F in mainline rtw88, which matches none of the explicit switch cases in rtw8821c_read_efuse() (0x2/0x4/0x7/0xa/0xc/0xf) so hal->rfe_btg stays false, get_cck_rx_pwr() falls into the 'else' LNA gain table branch, and rtw8821c_coex_cfg_rfe_type()'s switch on rfe_module_type=0x1F hits whatever its default case is (not individually enumerated here) instead of the correct antenna/RFE-module case. Net effect: silently wrong RX LNA gain table selection and wrong BT-coexistence antenna/RFE-module configuration — degraded RX sensitivity and/or wrong BT-coex antenna switching, NOT a bind/probe failure and NOT boot-critical. The device still binds (its USB ID is in the mainline table) and should still associate to WiFi; the regression is silent RF/coex misconfiguration, not non-functionality.
affected_hardware: EDUP EP-AC1661 (named in the fork commit message as the motivating device with a known-bad EFUSE batch)

notes:
sonnet-verified (ESCALATION review, redone from scratch):

(1) FORK COMMIT — confirmed via `git -C /mnt/source/Linux-Kernel_MiSTer show fc09a292a97b7cb2c4e1f60d55615bc8df419061`. Exact added hunk in hal/rtl8821c/rtl8821c_ops.c Hal_ReadRFEType():
```
 hal->rfe_type = map[EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C];
 if (0xFF != hal->rfe_type)
     goto exit;
+
+// fix for bad EFUSE in EDUP EP-AC1661
+hal->rfe_type = map[EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C-1];
+if (0xFF != hal->rfe_type)
+    goto exit;
```
EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C is `#define EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C 0xCA` (include/hal_pg.h:518 in the fork). Confirms the record's claimed offset 0xCA→0xC9 fallback exactly. The Makefile hunk is unrelated debug-logging config (CONFIG_RTW_DEBUG/CONFIG_RTW_LOG_LEVEL) and does not affect this analysis. IMPORTANT: this code is NOT gated on any USB VID/PID — it is a blanket change to Hal_ReadRFEType() affecting every rtl8821c-family device that hits mapvalid && map[0xCA]==0xFF, not an EDUP-specific quirk. EDUP EP-AC1661 is only the motivating case named in the commit message; any 8821c unit with the same bad-efuse symptom would hit the same fallback.

(2) VANILLA rtw8821c.c — confirmed NO equivalent fallback. rtw8821c_read_efuse() does a single unconditional read `efuse->rfe_option = map->rfe_option & 0x1f;` with no 0xFF sentinel check and no secondary-offset fallback anywhere in the function or file (grep of 'rfe_option' across rtw8821c.c shows only the 6 use sites quoted above — none add a bad-value guard). CRITICAL NUANCE not captured in the first-pass record: I pulled the actual `struct rtw8821c_efuse` layout from rtw8821c.h:74-98 and counted offsets — the byte immediately preceding `rfe_option` (0xCA) is `rf_antenna_option` (0xC9), a DIFFERENT, semantically distinct EFUSE field (antenna option, not a redundant/backup copy of RFE type). So the fork's 'fallback' is not reading a legitimate secondary RFE-type storage location — it is reinterpreting an adjacent, unrelated antenna-option byte as an RFE type when the real byte is blank. This is a hacky, batch-specific heuristic (it happened to produce a plausible rfe_type value for the specific bad-EFUSE units Sorgelig had in hand), not a principled fix. This matters for anyone considering forward-porting it: it should not be blindly re-implemented as 'read offset N-1 as a generic bad-efuse fallback' without re-verifying against the actual bad-efuse dumps from real hardware.

(3) GIT LOG SEARCH in /mnt/source/linux v5.15.1..v6.18.38 on drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/: `--grep=8821c --grep=efuse --all-match` returns 6 commits (2c17afde9ff6, 74a72c367573, 8fbcaa308591, 0a999d82b782, 64e9d5646535, 5db4943a9d6f) — none add a bad/blank-efuse rfe_option fallback for 8821c. Separate `--grep=8821c` finds e1958f4d66a5 ('rtw8821cu: Add ID for Mercusys MU6H') — adding a new device ID, no efuse quirk. Separate `--grep=efuse` finds 2c17afde9ff6 ('wifi: rtw88: Handle RTL8723D(S) with blank efuse', May 2025) — this IS a real precedent for rtw88 handling blank/near-blank efuse (default TX power/antenna/crystal-cap values when the chip's efuse is missing them) but it is scoped to RTL8723D(S)/RTL8703B only (moved into a shared __rtl8723x_read_efuse()), and does not touch rtw8821c.c. So mainline is aware of and has fixed blank-efuse issues for a sibling chip family, but has never received an equivalent fix for 8821c's rfe_option. Confirms absence with high confidence — not merely an omission I failed to find.

(4) DEVICE BINDING — confirmed 0x7392:0xc811 and 0x7392:0xd811 are both present in the rtw8821cu USB ID table: `drivers/net/wireless/realtek/rtw88/rtw8821cu.c:36,38` — `{ USB_DEVICE_AND_INTERFACE_INFO(0x7392, 0xc811, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff), ... },` / `{ USB_DEVICE_AND_INTERFACE_INFO(0x7392, 0xd811, 0xff, 0xff, 0xff), ... },`. So a device with these IDs DOES bind to mainline rtw88_8821cu in our build. A bad efuse does NOT block binding/probe (severity is NOT boot-critical) — it produces silent RF/BT-coex misconfiguration as detailed in `impact.effect_if_absent` above. Caveat: I could not independently verify against an authoritative USB ID database that 0x7392:0xc811/0xd811 specifically ARE the EDUP EP-AC1661's registered IDs (no internet access in this sandbox) — that mapping is inherited from the first-pass record. The code-level regression finding (no fallback exists in mainline, for ANY 8821c device with this bad-efuse symptom) stands independently of whether this exact VID:PID pair is verified.

(5) BR2 PACKAGE CHECK — /mnt/source/Buildroot_MiSTer/package/rtl8821cu-morrownr/ EXISTS (github morrownr/8821cu-20210916 @ 7f63a9da2e8ed83403f6f920e9b1628a37b38ef4, GPL-2.0). I extracted the actual pinned tarball from dl/rtl8821cu-morrownr/ and read hal/rtl8821c/rtl8821c_ops.c directly: Hal_ReadRFEType() there is the UNPATCHED original — `hal->rfe_type = map[EEPROM_RFE_OPTION_8821C]; if (0xFF != hal->rfe_type) goto exit;` followed straight by the 'Empty EFUSE with unknown RFE type!!' error path — NO fallback to offset 0xC9. So the morrownr out-of-tree package does NOT carry Sorgelig's workaround either. Additionally this package is OFF by default: `configs/mister_de10nano_defconfig:570` — '# BR2_PACKAGE_RTL8821CU_MORROWNR is not set -> mainline rtw88_8821cu (8811cu/8821cu)', and `board/mister/de10nano/linux.config:488` confirms `CONFIG_RTW88_8821CU=m` is what actually ships. CONCLUSION: neither of the two available paths for this hardware in our current build (default mainline rtw88_8821cu, or opt-in package/rtl8821cu-morrownr) carries this fix. This does NOT change the regression story as hypothesized in the task — it STRENGTHENS it: there is genuinely zero coverage anywhere in this repo's build surface today.

SCHEMA/GROUNDING-CONTRACT CORRECTION: the first-pass record paired `upstream.equivalence="contradicted"` with `disposition="dropped-upstream"`. worker-instructions.md's Pilot Lessons explicitly forbid this exact pairing ('Never pair contradicted with disposition="dropped-upstream"' — contradicted must be treated as a carry candidate). Reclassified disposition to `needs-verification` here: the code-level facts are fully grounded (high confidence, no hardware needed to confirm the mainline/morrownr gap), but the disposition enum has no clean 'confirmed gap, pending carry decision' value, and a human decision is still needed on whether/how to carry this forward (most plausible path: a small patch to package/rtl8821cu-morrownr's vendored source for users who hit this specific bad-efuse batch, since that's the only in-repo tree with the matching vendor code structure — patching mainline rtw88 upstream would need re-deriving the fix against the real bad-efuse dumps given point (2)'s antenna_option/rfe_option nuance). Also cleared `dependencies.superseded_by` (previously named mainline rtw88_8821cu as superseding this commit) since 'superseded_by' implies functional equivalence per the pilot lessons, which is contradicted, not established, here.

SILENT REGRESSION: confirmed. failure_mode=silent, severity=feature-loss (not boot-critical — device still binds and functions, just misconfigured). Realistically affects only the small population of 8821c-family USB adapters with this specific bad-EFUSE symptom (evidently at least one EDUP EP-AC1661 batch); most users' hardware is unaffected since ordinarily map[0xCA] is programmed.

sonnet-verified: CONFIRMED with correction — the core claim (no equivalent fallback anywhere in mainline rtw88 or in our build's morrownr alternative) holds up under independent re-derivation, but the first-pass record violated the project's own grounding-contract pilot lesson (contradicted+dropped-upstream pairing) and understated the BR2-package angle (which, once checked, reinforces rather than undermines the regression finding). Disposition corrected to needs-verification; superseded_by corrected to empty; one new technical nuance surfaced (0xC9 = rf_antenna_option, not a backup RFE slot).
